commands.review_summary:
The PR replaces three usage-bar translator lookup checks with `Object.hasOwn` so alias and case tables only match own properties.

PR surface: Source 0. Total 0 across 1 file.

Reproducibility: yes. Source inspection shows current main checks user-authored alias and case tables with `in`, so a template value like `toString` can hit Object.prototype instead of the authored table or `_default`; I did not run tests because this review was read-only.

target.title: fix: use Object.hasOwn instead of in operator in usage bar translator
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where the `in` operator in the usage bar translator would match inherited prototype properties like `toString`, `valueOf`, or `constructor` on vocabulary alias tables and segment case tables, potentially returning incorrect translations.

## Why This Change Was Made

Replace `key in table` with `Object.hasOwn(table, key)` in two locations (alias vocabulary lookup and segment case table lookup) so only own properties are checked.

## User Impact

Fixes potential incorrect rendering of usage bar segments when vocabulary alias or case table keys coincide with `Object.prototype` property names.

## Evidence

The `in` operator traverses the prototype chain, which `Object.hasOwn` (ES2022) does not. This is the correct behavior for lookup tables that should only match explicitly defined keys.
target.author: zhanglei99586
